_meta.title: Spec 35 optimal-control registry — behaviour-family slice (11 rows)
_meta.note: Unlike css-property/input-type rows, each row here defines a control SET (control_set array) for a recurring UI behaviour, not a single control. optimal_control.component names the shared component that should own the whole set.

rows.label: Show/hide content-element toggle

optimal_control.component: ToggleControl (native) — one per content element, grouped under a 'Display' ToolsPanel; NOT a bundling component (each toggle is independent, unrelated content, unlike carousel/disclosure which share live state)

props.grouping: ToolsPanel + ToolsPanelItem per element, 1-3 isShownByDefault (the block's most-recognisable pieces, e.g. Title/Date)

optimal_control.reset_disclosure: ToolsPanel resetAll clears every show-toggle to its block.json default in one action (dimension 7)

control_set.member_label: Show <element> (e.g. Show avatar, Show date, Show rating)
dimensions_applied: dimension 7 (ToolsPanel disclosure once a block has 4+ show-toggles)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part A5 (ToolsPanel once ~6+ controls), Part A9 (in-row layout), Part H (segmented-choice component list — booleans stay ToggleControl)
rows.current_state: 31 distinct attr names () across 14 blocks, every one a bare boolean-toggle at attr_type=boolean with no shared panel structure or ToolsPanel grouping — each block hand-rolls its own flat list of ToggleControls.
rows.divergence: not a wrong-component problem (ToggleControl is already correct per attr) — the divergence is presentation: dense blocks (post-grid has 8+ show-toggles, trustpilot-reviews has 7+) render them as one flat un-grouped list instead of a disclosure-managed 'Display' panel (Part A5/F anti-pattern 'no headers past a handful').
rows.notes: Distinct from visibility-responsive (below): this family is CONTENT-ELEMENT visibility (which pieces of the block's own markup render — show avatar, show rating) — always-on regardless of device/state. visibility-responsive is WHOLE-BLOCK visibility (device tier, empty-state, editor-preview-only). Never merge the two families into one component; they answer different questions ('what does this block show' vs 'when/where is this block visible').

rows.label: Scroll-triggered / entrance animation

optimal_control.component: BUILD: ScrollRevealControls (extends the existing AnimationControl/AnimationPanel — src/components/AnimationControl.js + universal-extensions/AnimationPanel.js — to cover parallax/Ken-Burns/SVG-draw/stagger, which today live OUTSIDE it as bespoke per-block attrs)
optimal_control.preset_layer: animation-style presets (fade/slide/zoom/draw/Ken-Burns) seed a real ms-level delay/duration/stagger box underneath — never presets alone
optimal_control.units: ms (duration/delay/offset/stagger) — RangeControl with real min/max/step + input field, not unitless slider only
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ToggleGroupControl for animationStyle/scrollDirection/svgAnimation (short sets); Custom path n/a — closed enum by design (CSS keyframe names)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset + resetFallbackValue on every Range; ToolsPanel disclosure once >6 controls (parallax/Ken-Burns/SVG/stagger together comfortably exceeds this on trust-bar/hero/container)

control_set.component: informational Notice + automatic prefers-reduced-motion CSS gate (not a client-facing override — animation must self-disable, per WCAG 2.3.3)
dimensions_applied: Part C/E5 mandatory gate — NOT currently a registry attr; this is a GAP, see divergence

control_set.member_label: Stagger delay / reveal stagger / path-draw duration / trigger offset / scroll offset
Background motion (Ken Burns / parallax + strength / flip-X)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part C Motion/animation ('entrance P · scroll-triggered P · parallax P · prefers-reduced-motion gating on ALL animation T'), Part E5, Part I ('Reduced-motion gate — verify on animation ext')
rows.current_state: 22 attrs across 12 blocks. A universal AnimationPanel/AnimationControl already exists (sgsAnimation/sgsAnimationDelay/sgsAnimationDuration/sgsAnimationEasing, wired globally to nearly all sgs/* blocks via editor.BlockEdit filter) but it is a NARROWER shape — plain entrance fade/slide only. The scroll-reveal-animation family's 22 attrs (bgKenBurns, bgParallax+parallaxStrength, bgSvgAnimation+Speed, svgAnimation+Source+Speed, revealOnScroll+revealStagger, staggerDelay, captionReveal, fadeOnScroll, flipX, pathDrawOnScroll+DurationMs+TriggerOffset, scrollDirection/Offset/Spy, smoothScroll, animationStyle) are ALL block-private, not routed through the universal AnimationControl at all — every block (container, cta-section, hero, trust-bar, decorative-image, gallery, timeline, table-of-contents, media, card-grid, post-grid, team-member, testimonial, google-reviews, trustpilot-reviews, responsive-logo, brand-strip) reinvents its own subset with its own naming.
rows.divergence: 22 scroll/motion attrs are all block-private and bypass the existing universal AnimationControl, each block reinventing its own subset under its own names; and a per-block prefers-reduced-motion gate must be confirmed applied framework-wide (WCAG 2.3.3) before any consolidation.
rows.notes: MANDATORY: no member attr in setting-reclassification.json currently represents a per-block prefers-reduced-motion override — verify (per Spec 35 Part I 'ensure every animation is gated') whether the CSS gate is applied automatically at the framework/animation.js level (cause-agnostic, no client control needed) rather than exposing a toggle; if it is NOT applied framework-wide today, that is the #1 gap to close before any other scroll-reveal work, ahead of consolidating the 22 attrs. Do not add a client-facing 'disable animation' toggle as a substitute for the automatic CSS media-query gate — WCAG 2.3.3 requires it to fire without the client knowing to ask.

rows.label: Slider / carousel controls

optimal_control.component: BUILD: CarouselControls (shared set — no such component exists today; every slider-carrying block hand-rolls its own)

props.autoplaySpeed: RangeControl (ms, withInputField, allowReset, reduced-motion-aware — pause autoplay entirely under prefers-reduced-motion)
props.slidesVisible: RangeControl inside ResponsiveControl (device-tier: how many slides show per breakpoint)

optimal_control.units: ms for speed controls (autoplaySpeed/autoScrollSpeed/carouselSpeed/scrollSpeed all converge to the same ms RangeControl)
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: n/a (all boolean/range) except scrollSpeed which today is enum-select — should convert to the same ms RangeControl as autoplaySpeed for consistency, not a separate slow/medium/fast enum
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset on every Range; ToolsPanel once autoplay+arrows+dots+speed+slidesVisible are all present (5 controls, borderline — group under a 'Carousel' panel regardless)

dimensions_applied: Part E5 reduced-motion aware — pause when prefers-reduced-motion

control_set.component: ToggleControl (block-specific extras, not core to every carousel — keep as opt-in ToolsPanelItem, not always-shown)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part C Motion (parallax/scroll P), Part I ('Reduced-motion gate — verify on animation ext' extends to autoplay), Part H (bounded numeric -> RangeControl, segmented choice -> ToggleGroupControl)
rows.current_state: 18 attrs across 9 blocks, and this is THE clearest name-divergence case in the whole registry: the SAME 4 concepts (show-arrows / show-dots / autoplay-on-off / autoplay-speed) are reimplemented under 4 different naming schemes per block — 'showArrows'/'showDots' (google-reviews, testimonial-slider, trustpilot-reviews) vs 'carouselShowArrows'/'carouselShowDots' (gallery, post-grid) vs no direct equivalent on brand-strip/trust-bar (which use 'scrolling'/'autoScroll' + 'pauseOnHover'/'autoScrollPauseOnHover' + 'scrollSpeed'/'autoScrollSpeed' for a continuous-marquee variant rather than paged slides). 'autoplaySpeed' (google-reviews/testimonial-slider/trustpilot-reviews, number) vs 'carouselSpeed' (gallery/post-grid, number) vs 'autoScrollSpeed' (trust-bar, enum-select) vs 'scrollSpeed' (brand-strip, enum-select) are the SAME ms-duration concept under 4 names and 2 different control types (number vs enum).
rows.divergence: The same 4 concepts (arrows/dots/autoplay/speed) are reimplemented under 4 naming schemes and 2 control types (number vs enum) across 9 blocks; should converge on one CarouselControls component with a paged|marquee variant prop.
rows.notes: Two sub-variants exist and both should map onto CarouselControls: (1) PAGED carousel (testimonial-slider, google-reviews, trustpilot-reviews, gallery, post-grid) — arrows/dots/autoplay/slidesVisible all apply. (2) CONTINUOUS-MARQUEE ticker (brand-strip, trust-bar, icon-list, nav-menu) — no dots/slidesVisible (it scrolls continuously), but shares autoplay-speed/pause-on-hover/direction. Component should expose a `variant: 'paged' | 'marquee'` prop that shows/hides the paged-only controls (dots, slidesVisible, arrows) rather than forcing marquee blocks to carry unused paged attrs. autoplaySpeed/carouselSpeed/autoScrollSpeed/scrollSpeed all converge to ONE ms attr the moment CarouselControls exists — the enum-select variants (scrollSpeed, autoScrollSpeed) should become the same RangeControl, not stay a separate slow/medium/fast picker.

rows.label: Video / audio playback controls

optimal_control.component: BUILD: MediaPlaybackControls (audio + video variants) — matches core/video and core/audio's own attribute set for feature parity (memory sgs-block-feature-parity-with-replaced-core)

props.autoplay: ToggleControl (with an inline Notice that autoplay implies muted — browser autoplay policy)

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ToggleGroupControl for preload (3 short options) — currently only declared on audio (audioPreload), missing on video
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset on toggles; group under a 'Playback' ToolsPanel once poster+preload are added (6 controls for video)

control_set.component: ToggleControl (video only — audio has no mute-by-default concept)

control_set.member_label: Poster image (video only) — GAP, not in current attr set
dimensions_applied: Part C Media — 'video poster/autoplay/loop/mute/controls (N: core video attrs) T'

control_set.component: ToggleControl (or fold into native loading behaviour if WP handles it automatically for video — verify before keeping as a client control)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part C Media ('video poster/autoplay/loop/mute (N: core video attrs) T') + memory sgs-block-feature-parity-with-replaced-core
rows.current_state: 11 attrs across 3 blocks: sgs/audio (audioAutoplay/audioControls/audioLoop/audioPreload — has preload), sgs/media video (videoAutoplay/videoControls/videoLazyLoad/videoLoop/videoMuted/videoPlaysInline — NO preload; poster IS present: videoPoster + videoPosterId attrs + a working MediaUpload poster control in edit.js), sgs/gallery (enableLightbox — a lightbox toggle grouped here).
rows.divergence: sgs/media (video) is missing only `preload` vs core/video — poster is already implemented (verified in block.json + edit.js). Playback toggles are also named per-block (audio* vs video*) rather than shared. Should share MediaPlaybackControls.
rows.notes: CORRECTED post-council 2026-07-19: the earlier 'MISSING poster + preload — confirmed absent' was FALSE — videoPoster/videoPosterId + a working poster-upload control exist in sgs/media (edit.js PanelBody 'Poster Image'). The genuine gap is `preload` only (present on audio as audioPreload, absent on video). enableLightbox (gallery) belongs more naturally in a lightbox-specific control (Part C 'native lightbox') — flag for a future reclass. Feature-parity per memory sgs-block-feature-parity-with-replaced-core.

rows.label: Collapsible / expandable content (accordion, collapsible text, dropdown menu)

optimal_control.component: BUILD: DisclosureControls (shared set — accordion, accordion-item, collapsible-text, table-of-contents, mega-menu, pricing-table, business-info, product-faq-item all hand-roll their own subset today)

props.defaultOpen: ToggleControl (per-item) OR RangeControl/index-picker (container-level 'which item opens by default', -1 = none)

optimal_control.units: collapsedLines is a unitless integer (line count) — RangeControl min 1 max ~10, not a CSS length
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ToggleGroupControl for openOn/toggleStyle/labelCollapse (2-4 short options each)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: allowReset; group under a 'Disclosure behaviour' panel once collapsible+allowMultiple+openOn+style are all present

control_set.component: RangeControl or a compact index-picker (-1 = closed)

control_set.member_label: Is open (per-item persisted state, e.g. accordion-item, product-faq-item)
control_set.component: ToggleControl on the CHILD block, not the container
ToggleGroupControl (or an icon-pair picker if the block exposes custom open/close icons)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part H ('optional-controls group -> ToolsPanel/ToolsPanelItem'), Part B Selection row (ToggleGroupControl for 2-5 short options)
rows.current_state: 9 attrs across 9 blocks with no shared component: sgs/accordion (allowMultiple bool, defaultOpen number/index), sgs/accordion-item + sgs/product-faq-item (isOpen bool, per-item), sgs/collapsible-text (collapsible bool, collapsedLines number), sgs/table-of-contents (collapsible bool, defaultCollapsed bool — inverse-polarity duplicate of accordion's defaultOpen concept), sgs/business-info + sgs/button (labelCollapse enum — an odd pairing; button's 'labelCollapse' is really an icon-only-collapse-to-icon-button pattern, conceptually closer to a responsive-display toggle than a disclosure, worth a future reclass), sgs/mega-menu (openOn enum: click/hover), sgs/pricing-table (toggleStyle enum).
rows.divergence: 9 open/collapse attrs across 9 blocks with no shared component and inverse-polarity duplicates (defaultOpen vs defaultCollapsed); should share DisclosureControls.
rows.notes: sgs/button's labelCollapse is a borderline member — it collapses a text label to icon-only, not an expand/collapse-content disclosure. Keep it in this family per the source classification (do not silently move it without a registry-wide pass) but flag it in the eventual lint as a 'verify fit' case, not a hard-fail divergence.

rows.label: Link target / rel / download behaviour

optimal_control.component: SgsLinkControl (existing — LinkControl wrapper, built this phase per the input-type slice's 'input:url-link' row). This family must NOT get its own separate toggles; every member folds into SgsLinkControl's settings prop.

props.settings: [{ id: 'opensInNewTab', label: 'Open in new tab' }, { id: 'nofollow', label: 'Mark as nofollow' }, { id: 'sponsored', label: 'Mark as sponsored' }, { id: 'download', label: 'Force download' } — rel is DERIVED from the settings checkboxes, never a separate raw rel string field]

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: n/a — settings are checkboxes inside LinkControl's popover, not a standalone dropdown
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: clearing the link clears new-tab/rel with it (single clear action, no separate reset needed)

control_set.component: SgsLinkControl settings checkboxes, auto-composed into the rel string — never a raw text/enum rel field
dimensions_applied: dimension 5 — this IS the dropdown-completeness fix: rel today is a free enum-select per block rather than LinkControl's composable settings

control_set.component: Once the Part I 'whole-card link' overlay-<a> pattern (sgsBlockLink) is built, its new-tab behaviour should read the SAME SgsLinkControl settings object as the block's primary link — not a second bespoke boolean
ToggleControl — kept SEPARATE, this is not a link-target setting (it swaps the href to the site home URL, a content-source decision, not a target/rel decision)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part B Link/CTA ('LinkControl — internal search + new-tab + rel'), Part C Links ('new-tab + auto-rel-noopener T · rel nofollow/sponsored T · whole-card/block clickable link T — high-impact gap'), Part F ('raw URL field instead of LinkControl')
rows.current_state: 8 attrs across 7 blocks, ALL still raw/bespoke: blockLinkTarget + sgsBlockLinkTarget (2 different attr names, same concept, on info-box/team-member — whole-card-link new-tab flag, no LinkControl involved since the whole-card-link overlay pattern itself is unbuilt per Part I), download (button, plain boolean), linkOpensNewTab (media, plain boolean), linkRel (icon/media, enum-select of nofollow/sponsor/ugc/noopener), linkToHome (responsive-logo, plain boolean, different concept — see notes), opensInNewTab (mega-menu, plain boolean), rel (button, enum-select). None of these route through SgsLinkControl's settings prop yet.
rows.divergence: 8 new-tab/rel/download attrs are raw/bespoke (2 names for one concept: blockLinkTarget vs sgsBlockLinkTarget); these settings belong inside SgsLinkControl's settings prop, not as parallel top-level attrs.
rows.notes: This is the input:url-link row's sibling — SgsLinkControl was already scoped as the shared component for the URL field itself; this row confirms the new-tab/rel/download SETTINGS also belong inside it, not as parallel top-level attrs. blockLinkTarget/sgsBlockLinkTarget (2 attr names for 1 concept across info-box/team-member) is a smaller internal duplication worth folding into one name once both blocks route through SgsLinkControl.

rows.label: Form field validation + conditional-display logic

optimal_control.component: BUILD: ConditionalFieldControl (the conditionalField + conditionalOperator + conditionalValue triplet) for the per-field conditional-display set; plain ToggleControl/RangeControl for the sgs/form-level security & behaviour settings (honeypot/rateLimit/requireLogin/storeSubmissions/multiSelect) — these are two DIFFERENT sub-groups inside one classifier family, see notes

props.conditionalField: SelectControl, dynamically populated from sibling field labels in the same sgs/form (not free text)
props.conditionalValue: TextControl, only rendered once an operator that needs a value is chosen (is-empty/is-not-empty hide it)

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: SelectControl for conditionalField (dynamic list, could grow past 10 siblings on a large form -> ComboboxControl) and conditionalOperator (fixed ~7 options, SelectControl is fine, ToggleGroupControl too wide for 7 labelled operators)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: conditionalValue only shows when the chosen operator needs one (progressive disclosure IS the reset/simplicity mechanism here, not a separate reset button)

control_set.member_label: Show this field only if… (sibling field)
control_set.component: SelectControl (ComboboxControl if a form has 10+ fields)

control_set.member_label: Honeypot / rate limit / require login / store submissions (sgs/form root — anti-spam + storage, not per-field)
control_set.component: ToggleControl + RangeControl (rateLimit is a submissions-per-window number)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part C Conditional/display ('by device T · by login/role T · by query/context P'), memory a11y-validation-feedback-informational-not-gate ('operator-facing a11y/validation fails = passive INFORMATIONAL editor/admin notices only; never a gate')
rows.current_state: 8 attrs (behaviour-family) + conditionalValue (classified separately as text-content, same functional triplet) across 14-15 form-field blocks. conditionalField is already correctly a dynamic-dropdown SelectControl per the classifier's 'why', conditionalOperator already a fixed-set SelectControl — both closer to optimal than most families. No shared ConditionalFieldControl component exists; each of the 14 form-field blocks re-implements the same 3-field pattern independently (14x duplication of identical logic, the highest per-attr duplication count of any family in this registry).
rows.divergence: 14 form-field blocks each re-implement the same conditional-field triplet independently (the registry's highest per-attr duplication); should share a per-field ConditionalFieldControl, distinct from the whole-block ConditionalVisibilityPanel.
rows.notes: This family is functionally DIFFERENT from the universal ConditionalVisibilityPanel (src/components/universal-extensions/ConditionalVisibilityPanel.js, sgsConditionLoggedIn/DateStart/DateEnd/UrlParam/Referrer) — that panel controls whether the WHOLE BLOCK is visible (login/date/URL/referrer), while form-validation-conditional controls whether ONE FORM FIELD shows based on ANOTHER FIELD's live value in the same form (sibling-driven branching logic). Do not merge these two into one component — they solve different problems and the second requires live sibling-field awareness the first doesn't need. Per the a11y-validation-feedback-informational-not-gate memory, any 'required field not filled' or 'conditional logic invalid' feedback in the editor must stay a passive Notice, never a hard block on saving/publishing.

rows.label: Sticky / shrinking header & nav-collapse behaviour

optimal_control.component: BUILD: StickyBehaviourControls — the sticky-position BOOLEAN should route through native `position: { sticky: true }` block support where the block declares it (site-header, notice-banner); the SGS-only shrink/transparent/collapse-tier behaviours have NO native WP equivalent and stay bespoke

props.headerSticky / stickyPosition: native position.sticky supports flag where available; ToggleGroupControl(top/bottom) for notice-banner's stickyPosition since it can stick to either edge
props.contrastSafe: ToggleGroupControl (auto/light-text/dark-text — safety net for a transparent header over varying hero brightness)
props.collapseTier / collapseCustomPx: ToggleGroupControl (768/1024/custom) + RangeControl(px) shown only when 'custom' is chosen

optimal_control.units: collapseCustomPx is a px RangeControl (device-tier px value)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: ToolsPanel — site-header alone already has 4 of the 7 members (headerSticky/Shrink/Transparent + contrastSafe), comfortably past the A5 threshold

control_set.component: native `position.sticky` block support (site-header, notice-banner) — prefer over a bespoke boolean per Part G
ToggleControl (no native equivalent — genuine SGS behaviour)

rows.shared_component: BUILD: StickyBehaviourControls (native position.sticky for the boolean; bespoke panel for the rest)
rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part G ('position.sticky N — HIGH priority, free inspector UI'), Part C Position/layout ('sticky (N: position.sticky) T')
rows.current_state: 7 attrs across 3 blocks: sgs/site-header (contrastSafe enum, headerShrink/headerSticky/headerTransparent bool — verified live: site-header/block.json declares NO `supports.position.sticky`, so headerSticky is a fully bespoke boolean today, not routed through the native support), sgs/adaptive-nav (collapseCustomPx number, collapseTier enum — this is a responsive nav-collapse BREAKPOINT, not stickiness, see notes), sgs/notice-banner (stickyPosition enum top/bottom).
rows.divergence: headerSticky is fully bespoke where native position.sticky exists (site-header declares no position support); the family also conflates true stickiness with the nav-collapse breakpoint — should split into a native StickyToggle + a NavCollapseTier.
rows.notes: The family name bundles two conceptually distinct behaviours the source classifier grouped together: (a) TRUE stickiness — stays fixed while scrolling (site-header's headerSticky, notice-banner's stickyPosition) — a native position.sticky candidate; (b) NAV-COLLAPSE BREAKPOINT — at what viewport width the nav switches from full menu to hamburger (adaptive-nav's collapseTier/collapseCustomPx) — a device-tier responsive concept, closer to visibility-responsive than to stickiness. Keep both under this one family per the source (do not silently split without a registry-wide pass) but the eventual shared component should likely be TWO small pieces (StickyToggle + NavCollapseTier) rather than one monolith, since site-header and adaptive-nav never both need the full set. | Spec 32 routing: adopting this native supports.* flag MUST pair with __experimentalSkipSerialization + wp_style_engine_get_styles scoped-<style> emission (the D292 pattern) — never a bare supports declaration (it auto-inlines).

rows.label: Hover state visual effects

optimal_control.component: StateToggleControl (EXISTS, src/components/StateToggleControl.js — the Normal/Hover segmented switch). Every *Hover attr must route through it: the control renders whichever underlying property control (colour/scale/shadow/decoration) the block already has, scoped to the selected state — never a second bespoke 'Hover Effects' panel duplicating the Normal panel.

props.swatches: legend showing both Normal + Hover values stay visible in both states (discoverability)
props.children: render-prop returning the SAME property controls used for Normal, just bound to the Hover-suffixed attr when state==='hover'

optimal_control.preset_layer: effectHover (scale/shadow/zoom/grayscale/overlay/decoration) acts as a PRESET selector that seeds which fine-grained Hover control(s) appear beneath it — not a replacement for the fine controls
optimal_control.units: scaleHover is a %/ratio RangeControl (e.g. 100-120), matching the existing sgsHoverScale pattern
optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ToggleGroupControl for effectHover/shadowHover/textDecorationHover (each a short closed set)
optimal_control.alpha_clearable: n/a (colour hover states inherit alpha_clearable from whichever colour control they wrap, e.g. DesignTokenPicker)
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: resetting Normal must not silently reset Hover and vice versa — each state's reset is independent, StateToggleControl keeps both value sets in memory regardless of which is on-screen

control_set.member_label: Effect type (which hover effect is active: scale/shadow/zoom/grayscale/overlay)

control_set.component: RangeControl, scoped inside StateToggleControl's Hover state
ShadowControl, scoped inside StateToggleControl's Hover state (shares the SAME builder as the block's resting-state shadow, not a separate Small/Medium/Large/Glow select)
ToggleControl (+ DesignTokenPicker for overlay colour if the block supports one)

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part F ('duplicate hover panels / hover split from resting'), Part I ('Normal/Hover state — StateToggleControl EXISTS (2026-07-18) — roll out to stateful blocks'), Spec 35 checklist item 12
rows.current_state: 7 behaviour-family attrs (effectHover/grayscaleHover/imageZoomHover/overlayHover/scaleHover/shadowHover/textDecorationHover) across 14 blocks — PLUS a SEPARATE, ALREADY-BUILT universal 'Hover Effects' panel (src/components/universal-extensions/HoverEffectsPanel.js, wired via src/blocks/extensions/hover-effects.js to every block) exposing sgsHoverBgColour/sgsHoverTextColour/sgsHoverBorderColour/sgsHoverScalePreset/sgsHoverScale/sgsHoverShadow/sgsHoverDuration/sgsHoverEasing/sgsHoverImageZoom. Neither system uses StateToggleControl (verified: HoverEffectsPanel.js imports DesignTokenPicker/SelectControl/RangeControl/ToggleControl directly, not StateToggleControl) — this is TWO parallel hover systems running simultaneously: the universal sgsHover* panel AND 14 blocks' own private *Hover attrs (scaleHover on 11 blocks alone).
rows.divergence: Two parallel hover systems run at once — the universal HoverEffectsPanel (sgsHover*) AND 14 blocks' private *Hover attrs — neither using StateToggleControl; migrate HoverEffectsPanel to StateToggleControl and retire the per-block duplicates.
rows.notes: This is the textbook Part F anti-pattern 'duplicate hover panels / hover split from resting', caught live rather than hypothetically: a block using BOTH the universal HoverEffectsPanel (sgsHoverScale) AND its own private scaleHover attr would show the client two different 'hover scale' controls with no indication they might conflict. Fix-shape: migrate HoverEffectsPanel itself to render through StateToggleControl (so Normal/Hover share one panel, one set of property controls), then retire the 14 blocks' private *Hover attrs onto the same StateToggleControl-scoped controls, deleting the duplicate universal-vs-per-block split entirely. This is the highest-severity consolidation in the behaviour-family slice by duplication count (2 systems x 14 blocks).

rows.label: Whole-block visibility (device tier, empty-state, editor-preview)

optimal_control.component: DeviceVisibilityPanel (EXISTS, src/components/universal-extensions/DeviceVisibilityPanel.js + src/blocks/extensions/responsive-visibility.js — already wired globally via editor.BlockEdit filter to every block). Device-tier hide/show must ALWAYS route here; a block-private hideOnMobile/showOnDesktop pair is the divergence to remove, not a legitimate alternative.
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: ToolsPanel, already applied (PanelBody today — upgrade to ToolsPanel once bundled with the non-device members below)

dimensions_applied: dimension 3 device-tier (768/1024 — matches D2's locked standard, verify sgsHideOnTablet's stated '768px to 1023px' help text against the 768/1024 lock)

control_set.member_label: Hide when empty (no content to show, e.g. empty cart)
control_set.component: ToggleControl — NOT a device-tier control, a content-conditional (belongs conceptually nearer form-validation-conditional's 'conditional display' set than device visibility)

control_set.member_label: Show placeholder/pending state in editor only
control_set.component: ToggleControl — editor-only preview aid, not a frontend visibility rule at all

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part D (Responsive UX — D1 device-preview vs per-block responsive attrs, D2 locked 768/1024), Part C Conditional/display ('by device T')
rows.current_state: 6 attrs across 4 blocks. sgs/decorative-image duplicates the universal panel directly: hideOnMobile + hideOnTablet (bespoke booleans) alongside the ALREADY-WIRED universal sgsHideOnMobile/sgsHideOnTablet/sgsHideOnDesktop — decorative-image is one of the few blocks that should simply DELETE its private pair and rely on the universal extension it already inherits. sgs/whatsapp-cta uses the INVERTED polarity (showOnDesktop/showOnMobile, 'show' not 'hide') for the same concept. sgs/cart's hideWhenEmpty and sgs/trust-bar's showPendingInEditor are not device-tier at all (see control_set notes).
rows.divergence: Per-block device show/hide duplicates the already-wired universal DeviceVisibilityPanel (decorative-image's hideOnMobile/Tablet is pure deletable duplication; whatsapp-cta uses inverted show* polarity); route all through the universal extension.
rows.notes: Distinct from show-hide-display-toggle (the first row in this registry): that family is CONTENT-ELEMENT visibility (which pieces of a block's own markup render, e.g. 'show avatar'); this family is WHOLE-BLOCK visibility (does the block render at all, and on which device). decorative-image's hideOnMobile/hideOnTablet is the single clearest 'delete this, the universal extension already covers it' case found in the whole behaviour-family slice — it is pure duplication with zero unique behaviour, unlike most other high-severity rows which need a genuine BUILD.

rows.label: Dismiss / close-and-remember behaviour

optimal_control.component: BUILD: DismissibleControl (small, 2-control set) — currently inline in sgs/notice-banner's own edit.js with no shared component, but the pattern (a client-dismissable UI element that remembers the dismissal) is reusable beyond notice-banner (e.g. a future cookie-consent block, or a 'permanently hide this promo' variant of whatsapp-cta)

props.dismissBehaviour: ToggleGroupControl (session/permanent — matches the existing block.json enum exactly)

optimal_control.dropdown_completeness: ToggleGroupControl for dismissBehaviour (2 options: session vs permanent) — currently confirmed as a SelectControl-shaped enum in block.json (`enum: ["session","permanent"]`), a 2-option enum belongs in ToggleGroupControl per dimension 5, not a dropdown Select
optimal_control.reset_disclosure: dismissBehaviour only shown once dismissible is toggled on — progressive disclosure via conditional render, not a ToolsPanel (too small a set to warrant one)

control_set.member_label: Dismissible (show a close/X control on the frontend)
Remember dismissal for (this browser session / permanently)
control_set.component: ToggleGroupControl, conditionally rendered when dismissible is on

rows.spec_ref: Spec 35 Part H (segmented choice -> ToggleGroupControl), Part A2 (sensible defaults — dismissBehaviour defaults to 'session' in block.json, correct default-to-least-permanent choice)
rows.current_state: 2 attrs, 1 block only (sgs/notice-banner) — verified live in block.json: `dismissible` (boolean, default false) + `dismissBehaviour` (string enum session/permanent, default session). No shared component exists because there is currently only one consumer.
rows.divergence: Single consumer (notice-banner) with a bespoke dismissible/dismissBehaviour pair; extract a small shared DismissibleControl now, and switch dismissBehaviour from SelectControl to ToggleGroupControl (dimension 5).
rows.notes: Lowest-severity row in the slice by consumer count (n=1 block), but still worth a named shared component now rather than later: the pattern is a natural fit for any future announcement-bar-style or promo-CTA-style block, and building it small now (2 controls) is cheaper than reverse-engineering a shared shape once a second block needs it and has already diverged. dismissBehaviour's enum value should switch from SelectControl to ToggleGroupControl per dimension 5 (2 short options) even though it is the only member with a genuine control-type gap — everything else in this row is already correctly shaped.
